value_moments.persona: Aisha, Site Coordinator at Helping Schools Kenya
value_moments.persona_pain_quote: Right now when a new child enrolls, I email a coordinator's spreadsheet to HQ — it takes 2-3 days before the child is in the system and visible to potential sponsors.
value_moments.persona_outcome: Aisha enrolls Daniel (8, Year 3 at Mwanza Primary, loves drawing and football) directly in Salesforce in under 2 minutes. He's immediately visible to the matching team.

wow_moment.description: After Aisha saves the child record, the page refreshes to show Daniel with all his site context (country, site coordinator, program level) on the related Site__c record — a fully formed sponsorship-ready profile, not a stub
wow_moment.why_audience_leans_forward: The audience expects bare-bones data entry. They see a complete, sponsorship-ready record with site fields, child fields, and relationships all populated. The 'when does the rest get filled in?' question never arises.
wow_moment.presenter_cue: Pause on the related Site__c. Note — this all came from one save.

anti_demo: Do not show the Site__c metadata or Object Manager
Do not show how the page layout was assembled
Do not show the field-level security settings

value_moments.persona: Joseph, Donor Engagement Specialist (Children Inc HQ)
value_moments.persona_pain_quote: When a donor wants to sponsor, today I'm switching between three systems to find an unsponsored child, create the recurring gift, and link them. It takes 15-20 minutes per sponsorship and I drop the link sometimes.
value_moments.persona_outcome: Joseph helps Margaret sponsor Daniel in under 90 seconds — picks an available child from a list, sets up a $40/month Gift Commitment, and the 1:1 link is automatic.

wow_moment.description: On the Gift Commitment save, the audience sees three things appear simultaneously: the linked child on the commitment, the linked donor on the child record, and the first scheduled Gift Transaction installment — the full GiftCommitment → GiftCommitmentSchedule → GiftTransaction relationship visible in one screen
wow_moment.why_audience_leans_forward: Children Inc's pain is that sponsorship is a multi-record dance done by hand. The audience sees the entire dance happen automatically from one save — and recognizes that the 'free hit' between Gift Commitment and Gift Transaction (the commitment exists before money arrives) is the right model.
wow_moment.presenter_cue: Watch all three records — commitment, schedule, first transaction. One save.

anti_demo: Do not show how Gift Commitment was configured
Do not show the auto-link Flow
Do not navigate to Setup or Object Manager
Do not explain the schema in admin terms — show the records, not the design

value_moments.persona_pain_quote: When a donor cancels, today I have to manually email the program team in Kenya to find out what to do with the child. Things slip through.
value_moments.persona_outcome: Joseph closes Margaret's Gift Commitment with a reason code in two clicks. A task automatically lands on the program team's queue with the child's name and the reason — they know to action it the same day.

wow_moment.description: Joseph clicks Cancel and picks 'Donor Financial Hardship' from the reason picklist. Cut to Aisha's queue in Kenya — the task is already there, with Daniel's name, the reason, and the cancel date.
wow_moment.why_audience_leans_forward: The audience sees a cancellation immediately become an actionable task in the right country, on the right person's queue. The handoff that used to lose children doesn't lose them anymore.
wow_moment.presenter_cue: Cut to Aisha's queue. Two seconds, the task is there.

anti_demo: Do not show the trigger or Flow that creates the task
Do not show how the reason picklist was configured

value_moments.persona: Joseph, Donor Engagement Specialist (handling the transfer)
value_moments.persona_pain_quote: Easy transfers when a child leaves the program is the #1 thing they asked for — today they call me, I dig through records, and three days later we have a new match. We lose donors during that gap.
value_moments.persona_outcome: When Daniel ages out of the program, Joseph re-links Margaret's Gift Commitment to a new child (Sarah, 7, same site) in two clicks. Margaret's giving never pauses. The transition note shows on Sarah's record.

wow_moment.description: Joseph clicks 'Transfer Sponsorship' on Margaret's Gift Commitment. A list of available children at the same site appears, ranked by sponsorship age and need. Joseph picks Sarah. The commitment re-points; Daniel's record shows 'Aged out — sponsored by Margaret Sept 2024-May 2026'; Sarah's record shows the new sponsor and the prior sponsorship history.
wow_moment.why_audience_leans_forward: The audience sees the most-asked-for feature work in 30 seconds. The system preserves history (what Daniel had) AND continuity (Margaret's giving doesn't pause). The thing that used to lose donors is now a two-click flow.
wow_moment.presenter_cue: Two clicks. History on both records. Margaret's gift never pauses.

anti_demo: Do not show the Transfer Sponsorship Apex/Flow source
Do not show the same-site matching logic
Do not show the history-preservation field configuration
